taxonomy.family_description: Grass family; cereals and grains - most economically important plant family

soil.type: Heavy clay or clay-loam soils with good water retention
soil.special_requirements: Impermeable clay layer needed for paddy cultivation; good drainage for upland rice

planting.spacing: 20×15 cm or 20×20 cm; 2-3 seedlings per hill

irrigation.method: Continuous flooding (5-7cm standing water) in paddy field

fertilization.schedule: Basal: 50%N+100%P+50%K; Topdressing: 25%N at tillering + 25%N at PI

harvesting.method: Reaping by sickle or combine harvester

symptoms.early: Small, oval to circular spots on leaves with yellow halo; dark brown margins and lighter centers
symptoms.late: Spots enlarge to typical oval brown spots, leaves yellow and die; glume discoloration leading to pecky rice

treatment.organic: Trichoderma seed treatment, adequate potassium nutrition
treatment.chemical: Mancozeb 75% WP (2g/L), Propiconazole 25% EC (1ml/L), Iprodione (1.5g/L)
treatment.preventive: Treat seeds with fungicide, correct soil nutrition (especially K and Si), use certified seeds, crop rotation

symptoms.early: Spindle-shaped (diamond) lesions on leaves with gray-white center and dark brown to reddish-brown margins
symptoms.late: Neck rot: lesion at panicle neck causes the head to break and hang down (Neck Blast). Grain blast: empty chaffy grains. Node blast: dark lesions at stem nodes.

treatment.organic: Spray Pseudomonas fluorescens (1% w/v), seed treatment with Trichoderma, avoid excess nitrogen
treatment.chemical: Tricyclazole 75% WP (0.6g/L), Isoprothiolane 40% EC (1.5ml/L), Kasugamycin 3% SL (1.5ml/L), Azoxystrobin
treatment.biological: Pseudomonas fluorescens, Bacillus subtilis, Trichoderma harzianum
treatment.preventive: Use blast-resistant varieties (BPT 5204, Swarna Sub1), balanced N fertilization, wider spacing for air circulation, destroy stubble

symptoms.early: Individual spikelets converted to small velvet-like greenish balls
symptoms.late: Balls enlarge to 10mm diameter, becoming orange-yellow then olive-green with black powdery spores, scattered through panicle

treatment.organic: Propiconazole-based sprays at booting stage
treatment.chemical: Propiconazole 25% EC (1ml/L) or Azoxystrobin at booting stage
treatment.preventive: Spray preventive fungicide at booting stage, avoid late planting, balanced nutrition

symptoms.early: Water-soaked to yellowish stripes along leaf margins, turning white to grayish, 'Kresek' phase in young seedlings (complete wilting)
symptoms.late: Milky, opaque bacterial exudate (dried beads) visible in morning on leaf tips, pale yellow 'straw' leaves, withered diseased panicles

treatment.organic: Drain fields; copper hydroxide (2g/L) as weak bactericide spray
treatment.chemical: Streptomycin + Tetracycline combination (limited effectiveness); Copper oxychloride preventively
treatment.biological: Pseudomonas fluorescens (preventive soil and foliar treatment)
treatment.preventive: Plant resistant varieties (IR64, Swarna), avoid excessive nitrogen, drain infected fields, destroy stubble

symptoms.early: Oval to elliptical greenish-gray lesions with white-gray interior and brownish border on leaf sheaths at water level
symptoms.late: Lesions coalesce and extend to upper leaves, white mycelial growth and brown sclerotia visible, complete sheath and leaf death

treatment.organic: Trichoderma harzianum or T. viride soil application (2.5 kg/ha), Pseudomonas fluorescens spray
treatment.chemical: Hexaconazole 5% EC (1ml/L), Propiconazole 25% EC (1ml/L), Validamycin 3% L (2ml/L), Carbendazim (1g/L)
treatment.preventive: Reduce plant density, lower nitrogen, drain standing water, use resistant varieties

control.organic: Clip leaf tips of transplanted seedlings (removes egg masses); light traps; pheromone traps; release Trichogramma japonicum
control.chemical: Cartap hydrochloride 4G granules (25 kg/ha in standing water), Chlorpyrifos 20% EC (2ml/L), Carbofuran 3G, Fipronil 0.3G
control.biological: Trichogramma japonicum (egg parasitoid, 1 lakh/ha at 10-day intervals), Tetrastichus schoenobii, Telenomus rowani

control.organic: Hermetic storage, diatomaceous earth, proper drying below 12% moisture
control.chemical: Phosphine fumigation, Pirimiphos-methyl grain protectant
control.biological: Limited; Theocolax elegans (ectoparasitoid wasp)

control.organic: Clean dry grain storage (< 14% moisture); diatomaceous earth 1.5g/kg mixed with grain; bay leaves in storage; neem leaf powder mixed with grain; hermetic storage (PICS bags, metal silos)
control.chemical: Phosphine fumigation (Aluminium phosphide tablets 3 tablets/tonne); Pyrethrin spray on empty stores; Deltamethrin WP on wall surfaces; Pirimiphos-methyl admixture
control.biological: No practical biological control for storage pests

pests.damage_type: Leaf and whorl feeding; ear damage

control.organic: Bt-kurstaki or Bt-aizawai spray; fall armyworm pheromone traps; sand+soil in whorl (suffocates larvae); egg mass and larvae hand collection
control.chemical: Emamectin benzoate 5% SG (0.4g/L into whorl), Chlorantraniliprole (Coragen), Spinetoram, Lambda-cyhalothrin (adult moths)
control.biological: Telenomus remus (egg parasitoid), Cotesia icipe, Coccygomimus turionellae, Metarhizium rileyi, NPV (Nuclear Polyhedrosis Virus)

planting.spacing: 22.5 cm row spacing (seed drill); broadcast is less preferred

irrigation.critical_stages: CRI (Crown Root Initiation at 21 DAS), Tillering, Jointing, Flowering, Grain filling

fertilization.npk: N120-P60-K40 for HYV; N150 with S15 for better protein

harvesting.maturity_indicator: Straw golden yellow; grain hard and moisture 18-20%

symptoms.late: Black teliospore pustules in late season, lodging of stems, shriveled grain

treatment.preventive: Rust-resistant varieties, eradicate barberry, prophylactic fungicide in Ug99-risk areas

symptoms.late: White-gray coating on all green parts, yellowing and drying of leaves, cleistothecia (black dots) visible in old patches

treatment.preventive: Resistant varieties, avoid dense planting, balanced nitrogen, timely fungicide sprays

symptoms.early: Stripe of yellow-orange uredia arranged in rows along leaf veins on upper leaf surface
symptoms.late: Extensive yellowing of leaves, black teliospores in stripes, reduced grain fill, shriveled kernels

treatment.preventive: Plant resistant varieties (HD-3226, WH1105), timely sowing to escape high-risk period, prophylactic fungicide at flag leaf stage

symptoms.late: Complete head replaced by black teliospore mass that disperses, leaving bare rachis

treatment.organic: Hot water seed treatment (52°C for 10 minutes)
treatment.chemical: Carboxin + Thiram seed treatment (2g/kg seed), Tebuconazole seed treatment
treatment.preventive: Use certified smut-free seeds, systemic seed treatment fungicides, plant resistant varieties

control.chemical: Phosphine fumigation (Aluminium phosphide tablets 3 tablets/tonne); Pyrethrin spray on empty stores; Deltamethrin WP on wall surfaces; Pirimiphos-methyl admixture
control.biological: No practical biological control for storage pests

symptoms.early: Chlorotic (yellow) streaks and mottling on younger leaves; pale green to yellow leaf discoloration
symptoms.late: Complete necrosis of all leaves from leaf tip, brown papery leaves, ear rot, premature death
symptoms.affected_parts: All plant parts, especially leaves and ears

treatment.organic: No cure; control insect vectors with neem oil, reflective mulch
treatment.chemical: No direct treatment; insecticides for vector control (Imidacloprid, Thiamethoxam)
treatment.preventive: Plant MLN-tolerant/resistant varieties, control thrips and aphids, crop rotation, early planting, remove infected plants

symptoms.early: Small, irregular tan spots with yellow halos, mainly on lower leaves
symptoms.late: Elongated, rectangular, grayish-tan lesions limited by leaf veins, covering entire leaf

treatment.organic: Copper-based sprays preventively
treatment.chemical: Azoxystrobin, Propiconazole, Pyraclostrobin at tasseling stage
treatment.preventive: Hybrid resistance, crop rotation, tillage to bury debris, fungicide at VT/R1

symptoms.early: Long (5-15cm) elliptical tan to grayish-green lesions with wavy margins on lower leaves
symptoms.late: Extensive leaf blighting from bottom of plant upward, dark olive-green spore mass on lesions in humid conditions

treatment.organic: Copper-based fungicides preventively
treatment.chemical: Azoxystrobin + Propiconazole (Quilt Xcel 1L/ha), Propiconazole alone (1ml/L)
treatment.preventive: Resistant hybrids, crop rotation, remove infected debris, timely planting

soil.type: Well-drained sandy loam to clay loam with good organic matter

irrigation.method: Drip irrigation preferred; avoid overhead (disease); furrow if drip unavailable

fertilization.schedule: Split N and K through fertigation (8-12 splits through season)

symptoms.early: Light brown water-soaked area at blossom end of green fruit
symptoms.late: Expanding dark brown to black leathery sunken patch at fruit base, secondary fungal colonization

treatment.organic: Foliar calcium chloride spray (4g/L), consistent irrigation, mulching, agricultural lime in soil
treatment.chemical: Calcium nitrate foliar spray (2g/L), drip application of calcium chelate
treatment.preventive: Consistent irrigation schedule, mulch to retain soil moisture, soil pH 6.2-6.8, avoid excess ammonium nitrogen

symptoms.early: Wilting during hot hours, poor plant vigor, chlorosis resembling nutrient deficiency
symptoms.late: Typical galls (root knots) on roots when pulled, stunted growth, reduced yield

treatment.organic: Marigold intercropping (Tagetes sp.), neem cake soil application, Paecilomyces lilacinus, Pochonia chlamydosporia

treatment.biological: Purpureocillium lilacinum, Trichoderma harzianum, Bacillus firmus
treatment.preventive: Crop rotation with non-host crops (maize, cereals), soil solarization, use resistant varieties (Mi-gene)

symptoms.early: Yellowing and wilting of lower leaves starting on one side of the plant, often one branch first

soil.special_requirements: Deep tillage for loose soil for tuber expansion

irrigation.critical_stages: Planting, tuber initiation (60-70 DAS), tuber bulking

fertilization.schedule: All at planting or split with K

harvesting.maturity_indicator: Vine death; skin doesn't peel off when rubbed
harvesting.method: Mechanical digger or manual fork; avoid damage

symptoms.early: Water-soaked pale green spots on leaves, white sporulation on underside in humid conditions
symptoms.late: Dark brown-black lesions on leaves, brown rots on stems, rapid spread of entire field collapse, tuber rot (brown-red internal)

treatment.organic: Copper hydroxide (2.5-3g/L) preventively, Bordeaux mixture, avoid excessive irrigation
treatment.chemical: Metalaxyl-M + Mancozeb 2.5g/L, Dimethomorph + Mancozeb, Cymoxanil + Famoxadone, Fluopicolide
treatment.preventive: Plant resistant varieties (Kufri Bahar, Jyoti), use certified disease-free seed tubers, apply fungicide before first rains, destroy volunteer plants

symptoms.early: No visible early symptoms on foliage
symptoms.late: Brown corky scab lesions (raised, sunken, or russeted) on tuber surface; external quality defect only

treatment.organic: Soil acidification (sulfur), crop rotation, adequate irrigation during tuber formation
treatment.chemical: Quintozene PCNB seed treatment (limited), Flutolanil seed treatment
treatment.biological: Fluorescent Pseudomonas, Bacillus subtilis soil inoculants
treatment.preventive: Lower soil pH to 5.0-5.5, maintain soil moisture during tuber initiation, rotate with non-susceptible crops (cereals), use scab-resistant varieties

control.organic: Yellow sticky traps, neem oil + soap spray (5ml+2ml per L), reflective mulch, soap water spray, Beauveria bassiana
control.chemical: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL (0.5ml/L), Thiamethoxam 25% WG (0.2g/L), Acetamiprid 20% SP (0.2g/L), Spiromesifen (resistance management)
control.biological: Encarsia formosa (parasitic wasp), Eretmocerus mundus, Macrolophus pygmaeus (predatory bug), Beauveria bassiana

control.organic: Metarhizium anisopliae fungus (1×10⁹ spores/ml) soil application; ploughing to expose and destroy grubs; light traps for adults; neem cake 250 kg/ha
control.chemical: Chlorpyrifos 20% EC (5L/ha) soil incorporation before planting; Carbofuran 3G (25 kg/ha); Phorate 10G; Imidacloprid seed treatment
control.biological: Metarhizium anisopliae, Beauveria bassiana soil inoculants; Heterorhabditis bacteriophora (entomopathogenic nematode)

pests.scientific_name: Amrasca biguttula biguttula (Cotton/Okra Leafhopper); Empoasca spp.

control.organic: Neem seed kernel extract 5%, neem oil; yellow sticky traps; Beauveria bassiana spray
control.chemical: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL (0.3ml/L), Lambda-cyhalothrin 5% EC (1ml/L), Thiamethoxam, Dimethoate 30% EC (2ml/L)
control.biological: Anagrus atomus (egg parasitoid in grapes), Gonatocerus ashmeadi, Stethorus ladybugs

treatment.organic: Remove infected tissue, improve air circulation, reduce humidity; Bacillus subtilis, Trichoderma spray
treatment.chemical: Iprodione (1.5g/L), Pyrimethanil (1ml/L), Fenhexamid (1g/L), Cyprodinil (0.5g/L). Rotate fungicides for resistance management.

pests.scientific_name: Thrips palmi (Melon Thrips); Frankliniella occidentalis (Western Flower Thrips)

control.biological: Neoseiulus cucumeris, Amblyseius swirskii (predatory mites); Orius insidiosus (pirate bug); Steinernema feltiae (entomopathogenic nematode) for soil pupae
